mission.title: Install and Configure Ratify on Kubernetes
mission.description: Ratify is an Artifact Ratification Framework that enables verification of artifact security metadata, ensuring that only compliant artifacts are deployed. Installing Ratify helps organizations enforce security policies and improve their deployment processes.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Ratify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add ratify https://notaryproject.github.io/ratify
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Ratify
steps.description:
Install Ratify using Helm in a new namespace called 'ratify'.
```bash
helm install ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --create-namespace --version v1.4.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Ratify pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ratify
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Ratify deployment to ensure it operates within defined constraints.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment ratify --namespace ratify --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Ratify Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall ratify --namespace ratify
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with Ratify.
```bash
kubectl delete crd ratifyconfigs.ratify.dev
kubectl delete crd ratifypolicies.ratify.dev
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'ratify' namespace to clean up all resources associated with Ratify.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace ratify
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of Ratify.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace ratify -o yaml > ratify-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ratify
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Ratify installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --version v1.4.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Ratify pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ratify
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Ratify pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace ratify
``` 
Look for configuration issues or missing dependencies in the logs.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace.
```bash
helm list --namespace ratify
``` 
If the release is not listed, it may not have been installed correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are being killed due to Out Of Memory (OOM), consider increasing the memory limits set during configuration.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace ratify
``` 
Adjust the resource limits accordingly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Ratify will result in the Ratify pods running in the 'ratify' namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add ratify https://notaryproject.github.io/ratify
helm repo update
helm install ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --create-namespace --version v1.4.0

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
